_title: My Experience Learning to Use AI for Software Development: Early 2023 to End of 2025
_description: A personal timeline of how my habits and thinking shifted as I went from casually experimenting with AI chat tools to making them a core part of how I build software.

_title: Set up Continuous Deployment to GitHub Pages with GitHub Actions
_description: How to create a GitHub actions YAML file that deploys the project to GitHub pages. Also touches on using environment variables in the pipeline.

_title: Initialize an NPM project with TypeScript
_description: How to initialize an npm & TypeScript project. Goes over configuration files for both.
Explains how to set up the different npm scripts and the commands needed when publishing a package to npm.
Notes about different topics in statistics from the 'Practical Statistics for Data Scientists' book.
Notes about different design patterns from the 'Head First Design Patterns' book.

_title: Publish NPM package from GitHub Actions
_description: Explains how to set up a manually triggerable GitHub Actions file that bumps the patch, minor, or major version of an NPM package and publishes it to the NPM registry.

_title: Track and Publish Test Coverage with CodeCov
_description: Explains how to track testing coverage with CodeCov and publish the results both from your local machine and automatically using GitHub Actions.

_title: Run GitHub Actions on a Schedule
_description: Explains how to configure a GitHub Action to run on a cron schedule.
Notes on topics related to linear regression.
Quick reference for common programs and commands used on the command line.
A high-level overview of the TensorFlow API.
How I like to set up my latex projects for writing papers.

_title: Speed Up Async Loops with Promise.all()
Classifying the MNIST dataset - The Hello, World of ML
Classifying the MNIST dataset - Additional Exercises
Titanic Dataset - Will a Passeger Survive?
Inside 13 Coding Agents: Control Loops, Tools, and Tradeoffs
_description: A source-code-level analysis of 13 open-source coding agents, comparing their control loops, tool sets, state management, and architectural tradeoffs.

_title: What Does Claude Think Is Architecturally Important?
_description: Analyzing 500 AI-generated architectural rubrics across SWE-bench Verified to see what a frontier model actually values in code architecture.

_title: Most of SWE-bench Verified Doesn't Require Deep Architectural Understanding
_description: Evaluating all 500 SWE-bench Verified instances for architectural complexity. 62% are trivial or low, and reasoning coordination consistently exceeds changes coordination.

_title: From Checklists to Prose Verdicts: Building an Architectural Evaluation Pipeline
_description: The development story behind 12 experiments building an AI-powered architectural evaluation pipeline, from brittle checklist rubrics to prose-based verdicts.

_title: My Experience Learning to Use AI for Software Development: Q1 2026
_description: Going deeper with Claude Code, building an observability tool, trying agent command centers, and discovering that most new tools don't stick.
